# Non-metric unit definitions

Imperial and US customary units have been redefined in relation to their respective common units. For example, the US gallon is defined as `<inch>, <inch>, <inch>`, while pints and quarts (both UK and US ones) are defined relative to their respective gallons.

This allows better clarity of unit composition, while also enabling second-order behavior, such as maintaining the same unit system when folding values (see: [exponents and roots](exponents-and-roots.md)) in presentation.

Note that imperial and US units are still fundamentally defined in metric units, so their conversion remains consistent. The change is for presentation only.
